name: James Leary
abstract: James Houston Leary III (June 4, 1946 – March 22, 2021) was an American double bass player and arranger/composer. Among his notable teachers and mentors was Ortiz Walton, the youngest member to sign with the Boston Symphony and its first African America member.

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: It was Eddie Marshall, James Leary, and Mark Levine on piano. ... It was a good band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: That was the last time I remember recording with him. He was the bass player.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hubert Laws — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Marshall
about_quotes.evidence: And so James Leary was the bass player. And so that lasted for five years.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Marshall interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Francisco, California, August 8, 2002
